title: Help creating scheduling board with hourly options
excerpt: Hi, I was wondering if anyone has any ideas on how I can achieve what I need to in a scheduling board. I need to create a scheduling board that will show me a schedule on a daily basis via the calendar view. I have 4 tim…

posts.author: Tara-Horn (Tara Horn)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, I was wondering if anyone has any ideas on how I can achieve what I need to in a scheduling board. I need to create a scheduling board that will show me a schedule on a daily basis via the calendar view. I have 4 time slots daily (each 2 hours). I would like to have the time slots to be seen on the calendar view. If I use a date column, it will show a start time, like 9am. There is no way to make that time slow into 2 hours. I tried with 2 hour columns for start and end, but it is also not showing up. Is there any way to get this into a calendar view? I will also need to have multiple people working at each time slot and I need to know who they are. Or is there another widget that I am not thinking about that can do this for me? This should be rather straight forward I would think.

posts.author: NRSSH (Nav)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to know this too.

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Tara-Horn @NRSSH , you might want to consider using the MasterPlan Calendar app.

It allows you to display your items as events with start and end date and hour, featuring color-coding based on status or people column values. You can also display several columns, such as people, location, links, text, and numbers. Additionally, it lets you connect multiple boards in a dashboard widget view.

I believe it could be a perfect addition for this use case.

https://monday.com/marketplace/10000472

gallery_image_11920×960 88.9 KB

Feel free to reach out with any questions
